verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācayad āttha māṁ mahā-bhāgatac-cikīrṣitam eva mebrahmā bhavo loka-pālāḥsvar-vāsaṁ me ’bhikāṅkṣiṇaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O greatly fortunate Uddhava, you have accurately revealed My desire to withdraw the Yadu dynasty from the earth and return to My own abode in Vaikuṇṭha. Thus Lord Brahmā, Lord Śiva and all other planetary rulers are now praying for Me to resume My residence in Vaikuṇṭha.

verses.sanskrit: मया निष्पादितं ह्यत्र देवकार्यमशेषत: ।यदर्थमवतीर्णोऽहमंशेन ब्रह्मणार्थित: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: mayā niṣpāditaṁ hy atradeva-kāryam aśeṣataḥyad-artham avatīrṇo ’hamaṁśena brahmaṇārthitaḥ
verses.text: Answering the prayer of Lord Brahmā, I descended within this world along with My plenary portion, Lord Baladeva, and performed various activities on behalf of the demigods. I have now completed My mission here.

verses.sanskrit: कुलं वै शापनिर्दग्धं नङ्‍क्ष्यत्यन्योन्यविग्रहात् ।समुद्र: सप्तमे ह्येनां पुरीं च प्लावयिष्यति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kulaṁ vai śāpa-nirdagdhaṁnaṅkṣyaty anyonya-vigrahātsamudraḥ saptame hy enāṁpurīṁ ca plāvayiṣyati
verses.text: Now due to the brāhmaṇas’ curse the Yadu dynasty will certainly perish by fighting among themselves; and on the seventh day from today the ocean will rise up and inundate this city of Dvārakā.

verses.sanskrit: यर्ह्येवायं मया त्यक्तो लोकोऽयं नष्टमङ्गल: ।भविष्यत्यचिरात्साधो कलिनापि निराकृत: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yarhy evāyaṁ mayā tyaktoloko ’yaṁ naṣṭa-maṅgalaḥbhaviṣyaty acirāt sādhokalināpi nirākṛtaḥ
verses.text: O saintly Uddhava, in the near future I will abandon this earth. Then, being overwhelmed by the Age of Kali, the earth will be bereft of all piety.

verses.sanskrit: न वस्तव्यं त्वयैवेह मया त्यक्ते महीतले ।जनोऽभद्ररुचिर्भद्र भविष्यति कलौ युगे ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: na vastavyaṁ tvayaivehamayā tyakte mahī-talejano ’bhadra-rucir bhadrabhaviṣyati kalau yuge
verses.text: My dear Uddhava, you should not remain here on the earth once I have abandoned this world. My dear devotee, you are sinless, but in Kali-yuga the people will be addicted to all types of sinful activities; therefore do not stay here.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु सर्वं परित्यज्य स्‍नेहं स्वजनबन्धुषु ।मय्यावेश्य मन: सम्यक् समद‍ृग् विचरस्व गाम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ tu sarvaṁ parityajyasnehaṁ sva-jana-bandhuṣumayy āveśya manaḥ saṁyaksama-dṛg vicarasva gām
verses.text: Now you should completely give up all attachment to your personal friends and relatives and fix your mind on Me. Thus being always conscious of Me, you should observe all things with equal vision and wander throughout the earth.

verses.sanskrit: यदिदं मनसा वाचा चक्षुर्भ्यां श्रवणादिभि: ।नश्वरं गृह्यमाणं च विद्धि मायामनोमयम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad idaṁ manasā vācācakṣurbhyāṁ śravaṇādibhiḥnaśvaraṁ gṛhyamāṇaṁ caviddhi māyā-mano-mayam
verses.text: My dear Uddhava, the material universe that you perceive through your mind, speech, eyes, ears and other senses is an illusory creation that one imagines to be real due to the influence of māyā. In fact, you should know that all of the objects of the material senses are temporary.

verses.sanskrit: पुंसोऽयुक्तस्य नानार्थो भ्रम: स गुणदोषभाक् ।कर्माकर्मविकर्मेति गुणदोषधियो भिदा ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: puṁso ’yuktasya nānārthobhramaḥ sa guṇa-doṣa-bhākkarmākarma-vikarmetiguṇa-doṣa-dhiyo bhidā
verses.text: One whose consciousness is bewildered by illusion perceives many differences in value and meaning among material objects. Thus one engages constantly on the platform of material good and evil and is bound by such conceptions. Absorbed in material duality, such a person contemplates the performance of compulsory duties, nonperformance of such duties and performance of forbidden activities.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् युक्तेन्द्रियग्रामो युक्तचित्त इदम् जगत् ।आत्मनीक्षस्व विततमात्मानं मय्यधीश्वरे ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād yuktendriya-grāmoyukta-citta idaṁ jagatātmanīkṣasva vitatamātmānaṁ mayy adhīśvare
verses.text: Therefore, bringing all your senses under control and thus subduing the mind, you should see the entire world as situated within the self, who is expanded everywhere, and you should also see this individual self within Me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानविज्ञानसंयुक्त आत्मभूत: शरीरिणाम् ।आत्मानुभवतुष्टात्मा नान्तरायैर्विहन्यसे ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: jñāna-vijñāna-saṁyuktaātma-bhūtaḥ śarīriṇāmātmānubhava-tuṣṭātmānāntarāyair vihanyase
verses.text: Being fully endowed with conclusive knowledge of the Vedas and having realized the ultimate purpose of such knowledge in practice, you will be able to perceive the pure self, and thus your mind will be satisfied. At that time you will become dear to all living beings, headed by the demigods, and you will never be hampered by any disturbance in life.

verses.sanskrit: दोषबुद्ध्योभयातीतो निषेधान्न निवर्तते ।गुणबुद्ध्या च विहितं न करोति यथार्भक: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: doṣa-buddhyobhayātītoniṣedhān na nivartateguṇa-buddhyā ca vihitaṁna karoti yathārbhakaḥ
verses.text: One who has transcended material good and evil automatically acts in accordance with religious injunctions and avoids forbidden activities. The self-realized person does this spontaneously, like an innocent child, and not because he is thinking in terms of material good and evil.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभूतसुहृच्छान्तो ज्ञानविज्ञाननिश्चय: ।पश्यन् मदात्मकं विश्वं न विपद्येत वै पुन: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūta-suhṛc chāntojñāna-vijñāna-niścayaḥpaśyan mad-ātmakaṁ viśvaṁna vipadyeta vai punaḥ
verses.text: One who is the kind well-wisher of all living beings, who is peaceful and firmly fixed in knowledge and realization, sees Me within all things. Such a person never again falls down into the cycle of birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइत्यादिष्टो भगवता महाभागवतो नृप ।उद्धव: प्रणिपत्याह तत्त्वंजिज्ञासुरच्युतम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaity ādiṣṭo bhagavatāmahā-bhāgavato nṛpauddhavaḥ praṇipatyāhatattvaṁ jijñāsur acyutam
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmi said: O King, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Kṛṣṇa, thus instructed His pure devotee Uddhava, who was eager to receive knowledge from the Lord. Uddhava then offered obeisances to the Lord and spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीउद्धव उवाचयोगेश योगविन्यास योगात्मन् योगसम्भव ।नि:श्रेयसाय मे प्रोक्तस्त्याग: सन्न्यासलक्षण: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvācayogeśa yoga-vinyāsayogātman yoga-sambhavaniḥśreyasāya me proktastyāgaḥ sannyāsa-lakṣaṇaḥ
verses.text: Śrī Uddhava said: My dear Lord, You alone award the results of yoga practice, and You are so kind that by Your own influence You distribute the perfection of yoga to Your devotee. Thus You are the Supreme Soul who is realized through yoga, and it is You who are the origin of all mystic power. For my supreme benefit You have explained the procedure for giving up the material world through the process of sannyāsa, or renunciation.

verses.sanskrit: त्यागोऽयं दुष्करो भूमन् कामानां विषयात्मभि: ।सुतरां त्वयि सर्वात्मन्नभक्तैरिति मे मति: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tyāgo ’yaṁ duṣkaro bhūmankāmānāṁ viṣayātmabhiḥsutarāṁ tvayi sarvātmannabhaktair iti me matiḥ
verses.text: My dear Lord, O Supreme Soul, for those whose minds are attached to sense gratification, and especially for those bereft of devotion unto You, such renunciation of material enjoyment is most difficult to perform. That is my opinion.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽहं ममाहमिति मूढमतिर्विगाढ-स्त्वन्मायया विरचितात्मनि सानुबन्धे ।तत्त्वञ्जसा निगदितं भवता यथाहंसंसाधयामि भगवन्ननुशाधि भृत्यम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’haṁ mamāham iti mūḍha-matir vigāḍhastvan-māyayā viracitātmani sānubandhetat tv añjasā nigaditaṁ bhavatā yathāhaṁsaṁsādhayāmi bhagavann anuśādhi bhṛtyam
verses.text: O my Lord, I myself am most foolish because my consciousness is merged in the material body and bodily relations, which are all manufactured by Your illusory energy. Thus I am thinking, “I am this body, and all of these relatives are mine.” Therefore, my Lord, please instruct Your poor servant. Please tell me how I can very easily carry out Your instructions.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यस्य ते स्वद‍ृश आत्मन आत्मनोऽन्यंवक्तारमीश विबुधेष्वपि नानुचक्षे ।सर्वे विमोहितधियस्तव माययेमेब्रह्मादयस्तनुभृतो बहिरर्थभावा: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: satyasya te sva-dṛśa ātmana ātmano ’nyaṁvaktāram īśa vibudheṣv api nānucakṣesarve vimohita-dhiyas tava māyayemebrahmādayas tanu-bhṛto bahir-artha-bhāvāḥ
verses.text: My dear Lord, You are the Absolute Truth, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and You reveal Yourself to Your devotees. Besides Your Lordship, I do not see anyone who can actually explain perfect knowledge to me. Such a perfect teacher is not to be found even among the demigods in heaven. Indeed, all of the demigods, headed by Lord Brahmā, are bewildered by Your illusory potency. They are conditioned souls who accept their own material bodies and bodily expansions to be the highest truth.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् भवन्तमनवद्यमनन्तपारंसर्वज्ञमीश्वरमकुण्ठविकुण्ठधिष्ण्यम् ।निर्विण्णधीरहमु हे वृजिनाभितप्तोनारायणं नरसखं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād bhavantam anavadyam ananta-pāraṁsarva-jñam īśvaram akuṇṭha-vikuṇṭha-dhiṣṇyamnirviṇṇa-dhīr aham u he vṛjinābhitaptonārāyaṇaṁ nara-sakhaṁ śaraṇaṁ prapadye
verses.text: Therefore, O Lord, feeling weary of material life and tormented by its distresses, I now surrender unto You because You are the perfect master. You are the unlimited, all-knowing Supreme Personality of Godhead, whose spiritual abode in Vaikuṇṭha is free from all disturbances. In fact, You are known as Nārāyaṇa, the true friend of all living beings.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचप्रायेण मनुजा लोके लोकतत्त्वविचक्षणा: ।समुद्धरन्ति ह्यात्मानमात्मनैवाशुभाशयात् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācaprāyeṇa manujā lokeloka-tattva-vicakṣaṇāḥsamuddharanti hy ātmānamātmanaivāśubhāśayāt
verses.text: The Supreme Lord replied: Generally those human beings who can expertly analyze the actual situation of the material world are able to raise themselves beyond the inauspicious life of gross material gratification.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मनो गुरुरात्मैव पुरुषस्य विशेषत: ।यत् प्रत्यक्षानुमानाभ्यां श्रेयोऽसावनुविन्दते ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmano gurur ātmaivapuruṣasya viśeṣataḥyat pratyakṣānumānābhyāṁśreyo ’sāv anuvindate
verses.text: An intelligent person, expert in perceiving the world around him and in applying sound logic, can achieve real benefit through his own intelligence. Thus sometimes one acts as one’s own instructing spiritual master.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषत्वे च मां धीरा: साङ्ख्ययोगविशारदा: ।आविस्तरां प्रपश्यन्ति सर्वशक्त्युपबृंहितम् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: puruṣatve ca māṁ dhīrāḥsāṅkhya-yoga-viśāradāḥāvistarāṁ prapaśyantisarva-śakty-upabṛṁhitam
verses.text: In the human form of life, those who are self-controlled and expert in the spiritual science of Sāṅkhya can directly see Me along with all of My potencies.

verses.sanskrit: एकद्वित्रिचतुष्पादो बहुपादस्तथापद: ।बह्‍व्‍य: सन्ति पुर: सृष्टास्तासां मे पौरुषी प्रिया ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: eka-dvi-tri-catuṣ-pādobahu-pādas tathāpadaḥbahvyaḥ santi puraḥ sṛṣṭāstāsāṁ me pauruṣī priyā
verses.text: In this world there are many kinds of created bodies — some with one leg, others with two, three, four or more legs, and still others with no legs — but of all these, the human form is actually dear to Me.

verses.sanskrit: अत्र मां मृगयन्त्यद्धा युक्ता हेतुभिरीश्वरम् ।गृह्यमाणैर्गुणैर्लिङ्गैरग्राह्यमनुमानत: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: atra māṁ mṛgayanty addhāyuktā hetubhir īśvaramgṛhyamāṇair guṇair liṅgairagrāhyam anumānataḥ
verses.text: Although I, the Supreme Lord, can never be captured by ordinary sense perception, those situated in human life may use their intelligence and other faculties of perception to directly search for Me through both apparent and indirectly ascertained symptoms.

verses.sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।अवधूतस्य संवादं यदोरमिततेजस: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: atrāpy udāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanamavadhūtasya saṁvādaṁyador amita-tejasaḥ
verses.text: In this regard, sages cite a historical narration concerning the conversation between the greatly powerful King Yadu and an avadhūta.

verses.sanskrit: अवधूतं द्विजं कञ्चिच्चरन्तमकुतोभयम् ।कविं निरीक्ष्य तरुणं यदु: पप्रच्छ धर्मवित् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: avadhūtaṁ dvijaṁ kañciccarantam akuto-bhayamkaviṁ nirīkṣya taruṇaṁyaduḥ papraccha dharma-vit
verses.text: Mahārāja Yadu once observed a certain brāhmaṇa avadhūta, who appeared to be quite young and learned, wandering about fearlessly. Being himself most learned in spiritual science, the King took the opportunity and inquired from him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीयदुरुवाचकुतो बुद्धिरियं ब्रह्मन्नकर्तु: सुविशारदा ।यामासाद्य भवाल्ल‍ोकं विद्वांश्चरति बालवत् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-yadur uvācakuto buddhir iyaṁ brahmannakartuḥ su-viśāradāyām āsādya bhavāl lokaṁvidvāṁś carati bāla-vat
verses.text: Śrī Yadu said: O brāhmaṇa, I see that you are not engaged in any practical religious activity, and yet you have acquired a most expert understanding of all things and all people within this world. Kindly tell me, sir, how did you acquire this extraordinary intelligence, and why are you traveling freely throughout the world behaving as if you were a child?

verses.sanskrit: प्रायो धर्मार्थकामेषु विवित्सायां च मानवा: ।हेतुनैव समीहन्त आयुषो यशस: श्रिय: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāyo dharmārtha-kāmeṣuvivitsāyāṁ ca mānavāḥhetunaiva samīhantaāyuṣo yaśasaḥ śriyaḥ
verses.text: Generally human beings work hard to cultivate religiosity, economic development, sense gratification and also knowledge of the soul, and their usual motive is to increase the duration of their lives, acquire fame and enjoy material opulence.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु कल्प: कविर्दक्ष: सुभगोऽमृतभाषण: ।न कर्ता नेहसे किञ्चिज्जडोन्मत्तपिशाचवत् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ tu kalpaḥ kavir dakṣaḥsu-bhago ’mṛta-bhāṣaṇaḥna kartā nehase kiñcijjaḍonmatta-piśāca-vat
verses.text: You, however, although capable, learned, expert, handsome and most eloquent, are not engaged in doing anything, nor do you desire anything; rather, you appear stupefied and maddened as if you were a ghostly creature.

verses.sanskrit: जनेषु दह्यमानेषु कामलोभदवाग्निना ।न तप्यसेऽग्निना मुक्तो गङ्गाम्भ:स्थ इव द्विप: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: janeṣu dahyamāneṣukāma-lobha-davāgnināna tapyase ’gninā muktogaṅgāmbhaḥ-stha iva dvipaḥ
verses.text: Although all people within the material world are burning in the great forest fire of lust and greed, you remain free and are not burned by that fire. You are just like an elephant who takes shelter from a forest fire by standing within the water of the Ganges River.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं हि न: पृच्छतां ब्रह्मन्नात्मन्यानन्दकारणम् ।ब्रूहि स्पर्शविहीनस्य भवत: केवलात्मन: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ hi naḥ pṛcchatāṁ brahmannātmany ānanda-kāraṇambrūhi sparśa-vihīnasyabhavataḥ kevalātmanaḥ
verses.text: O brāhmaṇa, we see that you are devoid of any contact with material enjoyment and that you are traveling alone, without any companions or family members. Therefore, because we are sincerely inquiring from you, please tell us the cause of the great ecstasy that you are feeling within yourself.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचयदुनैवं महाभागो ब्रह्मण्येन सुमेधसा ।पृष्ट: सभाजित: प्राह प्रश्रयावनतं द्विज: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācayadunaivaṁ mahā-bhāgobrahmaṇyena su-medhasāpṛṣṭaḥ sabhājitaḥ prāhapraśrayāvanataṁ dvijaḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa continued: The intelligent King Yadu, always respectful to the brāhmaṇas, waited with bowed head as the brāhmaṇa, pleased with the King’s attitude, began to reply.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीब्राह्मण उवाचसन्ति मे गुरवो राजन् बहवो बुद्ध्युपाश्रिता: ।यतो बुद्धिमुपादाय मुक्तोऽटामीह तान् श‍ृणु ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-brāhmaṇa uvācasanti me guravo rājanbahavo buddhy-upāśritāḥyato buddhim upādāyamukto ’ṭāmīha tān śṛṇu
verses.text: The brāhmaṇa said: My dear King, with my intelligence I have taken shelter of many spiritual masters. Having gained transcendental understanding from them, I now wander about the earth in a liberated condition. Please listen as I describe them to you.

verses.sanskrit: पृथिवी वायुराकाशमापोऽग्निश्चन्द्रमा रवि: ।कपोतोऽजगर: सिन्धु: पतङ्गो मधुकृद् गज: ॥ ३३ ॥मधुहाहरिणो मीन: पिङ्गला कुररोऽर्भक: ।कुमारी शरकृत् सर्प ऊर्णनाभि: सुपेशकृत् ॥ ३४ ॥एते मे गुरवो राजन् चतुर्विंशतिराश्रिता: ।शिक्षा वृत्तिभिरेतेषामन्वशिक्षमिहात्मन: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: pṛthivī vāyur ākāśamāpo ’gniś candramā raviḥkapoto ’jagaraḥ sindhuḥpataṅgo madhukṛd gajaḥ
verses.text: O King, I have taken shelter of twenty-four gurus, who are the following: the earth, air, sky, water, fire, moon, sun, pigeon and python; the sea, moth, honeybee, elephant and honey thief; the deer, the fish, the prostitute Piṅgalā, the kurara bird and the child; and the young girl, arrow maker, serpent, spider and wasp. My dear King, by studying their activities I have learned the science of the self.

verses.sanskrit: यतो यदनुशिक्षामि यथा वा नाहुषात्मज ।तत्तथा पुरुषव्याघ्र निबोध कथयामि ते ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yato yad anuśikṣāmiyathā vā nāhuṣātmajatat tathā puruṣa-vyāghranibodha kathayāmi te
verses.text: Please listen, O son of Mahārāja Yayāti, O tiger among men, as I explain to you what I have learned from each of these gurus.

verses.sanskrit: भूतैराक्रम्यमाणोऽपि धीरो दैववशानुगै: ।तद् विद्वान्न चलेन्मार्गादन्वशिक्षं क्षितेर्व्रतम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhūtair ākramyamāṇo ’pidhīro daiva-vaśānugaiḥtad vidvān na calen mārgādanvaśikṣaṁ kṣiter vratam
verses.text: A sober person, even when harassed by other living beings, should understand that his aggressors are acting helplessly under the control of God, and thus he should never be distracted from progress on his own path. This rule I have learned from the earth.

verses.sanskrit: शश्वत्परार्थसर्वेह: परार्थैकान्तसम्भव: ।साधु: शिक्षेत भूभृत्तो नगशिष्य: परात्मताम् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaśvat parārtha-sarvehaḥparārthaikānta-sambhavaḥsādhuḥ śikṣeta bhū-bhṛttonaga-śiṣyaḥ parātmatām
verses.text: A saintly person should learn from the mountain to devote all his efforts to the service of others and to make the welfare of others the sole reason for his existence. Similarly, as the disciple of the tree, he should learn to dedicate himself to others.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणवृत्त्यैव सन्तुष्येन्मुनिर्नैवेन्द्रियप्रियै: ।ज्ञानं यथा न नश्येत नावकीर्येत वाङ्‍मन: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṇa-vṛttyaiva santuṣyenmunir naivendriya-priyaiḥjñānaṁ yathā na naśyetanāvakīryeta vāṅ-manaḥ
verses.text: A learned sage should take his satisfaction in the simple maintenance of his existence and should not seek satisfaction through gratifying the material senses. In other words, one should care for the material body in such a way that one’s higher knowledge is not destroyed and so that one’s speech and mind are not deviated from self-realization.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेष्वाविशन् योगी नानाधर्मेषु सर्वत: ।गुणदोषव्यपेतात्मा न विषज्जेत वायुवत् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: viṣayeṣv āviśan yogīnānā-dharmeṣu sarvataḥguṇa-doṣa-vyapetātmāna viṣajjeta vāyu-vat
verses.text: Even a transcendentalist is surrounded by innumerable material objects, which possess good and bad qualities. However, one who has transcended material good and evil should not become entangled even when in contact with the material objects; rather, he should act like the wind.

verses.sanskrit: पार्थिवेष्विह देहेषु प्रविष्टस्तद्गुणाश्रय: ।गुणैर्न युज्यते योगी गन्धैर्वायुरिवात्मद‍ृक् ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: pārthiveṣv iha deheṣupraviṣṭas tad-guṇāśrayaḥguṇair na yujyate yogīgandhair vāyur ivātma-dṛk
verses.text: Although a self-realized soul may live in various material bodies while in this world, experiencing their various qualities and functions, he is never entangled, just as the wind which carries various aromas does not actually mix with them.

verses.sanskrit: अन्तर्हितश्च स्थिरजङ्गमेषुब्रह्मात्मभावेन समन्वयेन ।व्याप्त्याव्यवच्छेदमसङ्गमात्मनोमुनिर्नभस्त्वं विततस्य भावयेत् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: antarhitaś ca sthira-jaṅgameṣubrahmātma-bhāvena samanvayenavyāptyāvyavacchedam asaṅgam ātmanomunir nabhastvaṁ vitatasya bhāvayet
verses.text: A thoughtful sage, even while living within a material body, should understand himself to be pure spirit soul. Similarly, one should see that the spirit soul enters within all forms of life, both moving and nonmoving, and that the individual souls are thus all-pervading. The sage should further observe that the Supreme Personality of Godhead, as the Supersoul, is simultaneously present within all things. Both the individual soul and the Supersoul can be understood by comparing them to the nature of the sky: although the sky extends everywhere and everything rests within the sky, the sky does not mix with anything, nor can it be divided by anything.

verses.sanskrit: तेजोऽबन्नमयैर्भावैर्मेघाद्यैर्वायुनेरितै: ।न स्पृश्यते नभस्तद्वत् कालसृष्टैर्गुणै: पुमान् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tejo-’b-anna-mayair bhāvairmeghādyair vāyuneritaiḥna spṛśyate nabhas tadvatkāla-sṛṣṭair guṇaiḥ pumān
verses.text: Although the mighty wind blows clouds and storms across the sky, the sky is never implicated or affected by these activities. Similarly, the spirit soul is not actually changed or affected by contact with the material nature. Although the living entity enters within a body made of earth, water and fire, and although he is impelled by the three modes of nature created by eternal time, his eternal spiritual nature is never actually affected.

verses.sanskrit: स्वच्छ: प्रकृतित: स्‍निग्धो माधुर्यस्तीर्थभूर्नृणाम् ।मुनि: पुनात्यपां मित्रमीक्षोपस्पर्शकीर्तनै: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: svacchaḥ prakṛtitaḥ snigdhomādhuryas tīrtha-bhūr nṛṇāmmuniḥ punāty apāṁ mitramīkṣopasparśa-kīrtanaiḥ
verses.text: O King, a saintly person is just like water because he is free from all contamination, gentle by nature, and by speaking creates a beautiful vibration like that of flowing water. Just by seeing, touching or hearing such a saintly person, the living entity is purified, just as one is cleansed by contact with pure water. Thus a saintly person, just like a holy place, purifies all those who contact him because he always chants the glories of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: तेजस्वी तपसा दीप्तो दुर्धर्षोदरभाजन: ।सर्वभक्ष्योऽपि युक्तात्मा नादत्ते मलमग्निवत् ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tejasvī tapasā dīptodurdharṣodara-bhājanaḥsarva-bhakṣyo ’pi yuktātmānādatte malam agni-vat
verses.text: Saintly persons become powerful by execution of austerities. Their consciousness is unshakable because they do not try to enjoy anything within the material world. Such naturally liberated sages accept foodstuffs that are offered to them by destiny, and if by chance they happen to eat contaminated food, they are not affected, just like fire, which burns up contaminated substances that are offered to it.

verses.sanskrit: क्व‍‍चिच्छन्न: क्व‍‍चित् स्पष्ट उपास्य: श्रेय इच्छताम् ।भुङ्क्ते सर्वत्र दातृणां दहन् प्रागुत्तराशुभम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacic channaḥ kvacit spaṣṭaupāsyaḥ śreya icchatāmbhuṅkte sarvatra dātṝṇāṁdahan prāg-uttarāśubham
verses.text: A saintly person, just like fire, sometimes appears in a concealed form and at other times reveals himself. For the welfare of the conditioned souls who desire real happiness, a saintly person may accept the worshipable position of spiritual master, and thus like fire he burns to ashes all the past and future sinful reactions of his worshipers by mercifully accepting their offerings.

verses.sanskrit: स्वमायया सृष्टमिदं सदसल्ल‍क्षणं विभु: ।प्रविष्ट ईयते तत्तत्स्वरूपोऽग्निरिवैधसि ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-māyayā sṛṣṭam idaṁsad-asal-lakṣaṇaṁ vibhuḥpraviṣṭa īyate tat-tat-svarūpo ’gnir ivaidhasi
verses.text: Just as fire manifests differently in pieces of wood of different sizes and qualities, the omnipotent Supreme Soul, having entered the bodies of higher and lower life forms created by His own potency, appears to assume the identity of each.

verses.sanskrit: विसर्गाद्या: श्मशानान्ता भावा देहस्य नात्मन: ।कलानामिव चन्द्रस्य कालेनाव्यक्तवर्त्मना ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: visargādyāḥ śmaśānāntābhāvā dehasya nātmanaḥkalānām iva candrasyakālenāvyakta-vartmanā
verses.text: The various phases of one’s material life, beginning with birth and culminating in death, are all properties of the body and do not affect the soul, just as the apparent waxing and waning of the moon does not affect the moon itself. Such changes are enforced by the imperceptible movements of time.

verses.sanskrit: कालेन ह्योघवेगेन भूतानां प्रभवाप्ययौ ।नित्यावपि न द‍ृश्येते आत्मनोऽग्नेर्यथार्चिषाम् ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kālena hy ogha-vegenabhūtānāṁ prabhavāpyayaunityāv api na dṛśyeteātmano ’gner yathārciṣām
verses.text: The flames of a fire appear and disappear at every moment, and yet this creation and destruction is not noticed by the ordinary observer. Similarly, the mighty waves of time flow constantly, like the powerful currents of a river, and imperceptibly cause the birth, growth and death of innumerable material bodies. And yet the soul, who is thus constantly forced to change his position, cannot perceive the actions of time.

verses.sanskrit: गुणैर्गुणानुपादत्ते यथाकालं विमुञ्चति ।न तेषु युज्यते योगी गोभिर्गा इव गोपति: ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: guṇair guṇān upādatteyathā-kālaṁ vimuñcatina teṣu yujyate yogīgobhir gā iva go-patiḥ
verses.text: Just as the sun evaporates large quantities of water by its potent rays and later returns the water to the earth in the form of rain, similarly, a saintly person accepts all types of material objects with his material senses, and at the appropriate time, when the proper person has approached him to request them, he returns such material objects. Thus, both in accepting and giving up the objects of the senses, he is not entangled.

verses.sanskrit: बुध्यते स्वे न भेदेन व्यक्तिस्थ इव तद्गत: ।लक्ष्यते स्थूलमतिभिरात्मा चावस्थितोऽर्कवत् ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: budhyate sve na bhedenavyakti-stha iva tad-gataḥlakṣyate sthūla-matibhirātmā cāvasthito ’rka-vat
verses.text: Even when reflected in various objects, the sun is never divided, nor does it merge into its reflection. Only those with dull brains would consider the sun in this way. Similarly, although the soul is reflected through different material bodies, the soul remains undivided and nonmaterial.

verses.sanskrit: नातिस्‍नेह: प्रसङ्गो वा कर्तव्य: क्व‍ापि केनचित् ।कुर्वन् विन्देत सन्तापं कपोत इव दीनधी: ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāti-snehaḥ prasaṅgo vākartavyaḥ kvāpi kenacitkurvan vindeta santāpaṁkapota iva dīna-dhīḥ
verses.text: One should never indulge in excessive affection or concern for anyone or anything; otherwise one will have to experience great suffering, just like the foolish pigeon.

verses.sanskrit: कपोत: कश्चनारण्ये कृतनीडो वनस्पतौ ।कपोत्या भार्यया सार्धमुवास कतिचित् समा: ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotaḥ kaścanāraṇyekṛta-nīḍo vanaspataukapotyā bhāryayā sārdhamuvāsa katicit samāḥ
verses.text: There once was a pigeon who lived in the forest along with his wife. He had built a nest within a tree and lived there for several years in her company.

verses.sanskrit: कपोतौ स्‍नेहगुणितहृदयौ गृहधर्मिणौ ।द‍ृष्टिं द‍ृष्‍ट्याङ्गमङ्गेन बुद्धिं बुद्ध्या बबन्धतु: ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotau sneha-guṇita-hṛdayau gṛha-dharmiṇaudṛṣṭiṁ dṛṣṭyāṅgam aṅgenabuddhiṁ buddhyā babandhatuḥ
verses.text: The two pigeons were very much devoted to their household duties. Their hearts being tied together by sentimental affection, they were each attracted by the other’s glances, bodily features and states of mind. Thus, they completely bound each other in affection.

verses.sanskrit: शय्यासनाटनस्थानवार्ताक्रीडाशनादिकम् ।मिथुनीभूय विश्रब्धौ चेरतुर्वनराजिषु ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śayyāsanāṭana-sthānavārtā-krīḍāśanādikammithunī-bhūya viśrabdhauceratur vana-rājiṣu
verses.text: Naively trusting in the future, they carried out their acts of resting, sitting, walking, standing, conversing, playing, eating and so forth as a loving couple among the trees of the forest.

verses.sanskrit: यं यं वाञ्छति सा राजन् तर्पयन्त्यनुकम्पिता ।तं तं समनयत् कामं कृच्छ्रेणाप्यजितेन्द्रिय: ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaṁ yaṁ vāñchati sā rājantarpayanty anukampitātaṁ taṁ samanayat kāmaṁkṛcchreṇāpy ajitendriyaḥ
verses.text: Whenever she desired anything, O King, the she-pigeon would flatteringly cajole her husband, and he in turn would gratify her by faithfully doing whatever she wanted, even with great personal difficulty. Thus, he could not control his senses in her association.

verses.sanskrit: कपोती प्रथमं गर्भं गृह्णन्ती काल आगते ।अण्डानि सुषुवे नीडे स्वपत्यु: सन्निधौ सती ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotī prathamaṁ garbhaṁgṛhṇantī kāla āgateaṇḍāni suṣuve nīḍesta-patyuḥ sannidhau satī
verses.text: Then the female pigeon experienced her first pregnancy. When the time arrived, the chaste lady delivered a number of eggs within the nest in the presence of her husband.

verses.sanskrit: प्रजा: पुपुषतु: प्रीतौ दम्पती पुत्रवत्सलौ ।श‍ृण्वन्तौ कूजितं तासां निवृतौ कलभाषितै: ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: teṣu kāle vyajāyantaracitāvayavā hareḥśaktibhir durvibhāvyābhiḥkomalāṅga-tanūruhāḥ
verses.text: When the time was ripe, baby pigeons, with tender limbs and feathers created by the inconceivable potencies of the Lord, were born from those eggs.

verses.sanskrit: तासां पतत्रै: सुस्पर्शै: कूजितैर्मुग्धचेष्टितै: ।प्रत्युद्गमैरदीनानां पितरौ मुदमापतु: ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: prajāḥ pupuṣatuḥ prītaudampatī putra-vatsalauśṛṇvantau kūjitaṁ tāsāṁnirvṛtau kala-bhāṣitaiḥ
verses.text: The two pigeons became most affectionate to their children and took great pleasure in listening to their awkward chirping, which sounded very sweet to the parents. Thus with love they began to raise the little birds who were born of them.

verses.sanskrit: तासां पतत्रै: सुस्पर्शै: कूजितैर्मुग्धचेष्टितै: ।प्रत्युद्गमैरदीनानां पितरौ मुदमापतु: ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: tāsāṁ patatraiḥ su-sparśaiḥkūjitair mugdha-ceṣṭitaiḥpratyudgamair adīnānāṁpitarau mudam āpatuḥ
verses.text: The parent birds became very joyful by observing the soft wings of their children, their chirping, their lovely innocent movements around the nest and their attempts to jump up and fly. Seeing their children happy, the parents were also happy.

verses.sanskrit: स्‍नेहानुबद्धहृदयावन्योन्यं विष्णुमायया ।विमोहितौ दीनधियौ शिशून् पुपुषतु: प्रजा: ॥ ६१ ॥
verses.transliteration: snehānubaddha-hṛdayāvanyonyaṁ viṣṇu-māyayāvimohitau dīna-dhiyauśiśūn pupuṣatuḥ prajāḥ
verses.text: Their hearts bound to each other by affection, the foolish birds, completely bewildered by the illusory energy of Lord Viṣṇu, continued to take care of the young offspring who had been born to them.

verses.sanskrit: एकदा जग्मतुस्तासामन्नार्थं तौ कुटुम्बिनौ ।परित: कानने तस्मिन्नर्थिनौ चेरतुश्चिरम् ॥ ६२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekadā jagmatus tāsāmannārthaṁ tau kuṭumbinauparitaḥ kānane tasminnarthinau ceratuś ciram
verses.text: One day the two heads of the family went out to find food for the children. Being very anxious to feed their offspring properly, they wandered all over the forest for a long time.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा तान् लुब्धक: कश्चिद् यद‍ृच्छातो वनेचर: ।जगृहे जालमातत्य चरत: स्वालयान्तिके ॥ ६३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tān lubdhakaḥ kaścidyadṛcchāto vane-caraḥjagṛhe jālam ātatyacarataḥ svālayāntike
verses.text: At that time a certain hunter who happened to be wandering through the forest saw the young pigeons moving about near their nest. Spreading out his net he captured them all.

verses.sanskrit: कपोतश्च कपोती च प्रजापोषे सदोत्सुकौ ।गतौ पोषणमादाय स्वनीडमुपजग्मतु: ॥ ६४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotaś ca kapotī caprajā-poṣe sadotsukaugatau poṣaṇam ādāyasva-nīḍam upajagmatuḥ
verses.text: The pigeon and his wife were always anxious for the maintenance of their children, and they were wandering in the forest for that purpose. Having obtained proper food, they now returned to their nest.

verses.sanskrit: कपोती स्वात्मजान् वीक्ष्य बालकान् जालसंवृतान् ।तानभ्यधावत् क्रोशन्ती क्रोशतो भृशदु:खिता ॥ ६५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotī svātmajān vīkṣyabālakān jāla-saṁvṛtāntān abhyadhāvat krośantīkrośato bhṛśa-duḥkhitā
verses.text: When the lady pigeon caught sight of her own children trapped within the hunter’s net, she was overwhelmed with anguish, and crying out, she rushed toward them as they cried out to her in return.

verses.sanskrit: सासकृत्‍स्‍नेहगुणिता दीनचित्ताजमायया ।स्वयं चाबध्यत शिचा बद्धान् पश्यन्त्यपस्मृति: ॥ ६६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sāsakṛt sneha-guṇitādīna-cittāja-māyayāsvayaṁ cābadhyata śicābaddhān paśyanty apasmṛtiḥ
verses.text: The lady pigeon had always allowed herself to be bound by the ropes of intense material affection, and thus her mind was overwhelmed by anguish. Being in the grip of the illusory energy of the Lord, she completely forgot herself, and rushing forward to her helpless children, she was immediately bound in the hunter’s net.

verses.sanskrit: कपोत: स्वात्मजान् बद्धानात्मनोऽप्यधिकान् प्रियान् ।भार्यां चात्मसमां दीनो विललापातिदु:खित: ॥ ६७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kapotaḥ svātmajān baddhānātmano ’py adhikān priyānbhāryāṁ cātma-samāṁ dīnovilalāpāti-duḥkhitaḥ
verses.text: Seeing his own children, who were more dear to him than life itself, fatally bound in the hunter’s net along with his dearmost wife, whom he considered equal in every way to himself, the poor male pigeon began to lament wretchedly.

verses.sanskrit: अहो मे पश्यतापायमल्पपुण्यस्य दुर्मते: ।अतृप्तस्याकृतार्थस्य गृहस्त्रैवर्गिकोहत: ॥ ६८ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho me paśyatāpāyamalpa-puṇyasya durmateḥatṛptasyākṛtārthasyagṛhas trai-vargiko hataḥ
verses.text: The male pigeon said: Alas, just see how I am now destroyed! I am obviously a great fool, for I did not properly execute pious activities. I could not satisfy myself, nor could I fulfill the purpose of life. My dear family, which was the basis of my religiosity, economic development and sense gratification, is now hopelessly ruined.

verses.sanskrit: अनुरूपानुकूला च यस्य मे पतिदेवता ।शून्ये गृहे मां सन्त्यज्य पुत्रै: स्वर्याति साधुभि: ॥ ६९ ॥
verses.transliteration: anurūpānukūlā cayasya me pati-devatāśūnye gṛhe māṁ santyajyaputraiḥ svar yāti sādhubhiḥ
verses.text: My wife and I were an ideal match. She always faithfully obeyed me and in fact accepted me as her worshipable deity. But now, seeing her children lost and her home empty, she has left me behind and gone to heaven with our saintly children.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽहं शून्ये गृहे दीनो मृतदारो मृतप्रज: ।जिजीविषे किमर्थं वा विधुरो दु:खजीवित: ॥ ७० ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’haṁ śūnye gṛhe dīnomṛta-dāro mṛta-prajaḥjijīviṣe kim arthaṁ vāvidhuro duḥkha-jīvitaḥ
verses.text: Now I am a wretched person living in an empty home. My wife is dead; my children are dead. Why should I possibly want to live? My heart is so pained by separation from my family that life itself has become simply suffering.

verses.sanskrit: तांस्तथैवावृतान् शिग्भिर्मृत्युग्रस्तान् विचेष्टत: ।स्वयं च कृपण: शिक्षु पश्यन्नप्यबुधोऽपतत् ॥ ७१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁs tathaivāvṛtān śigbhirmṛtyu-grastān viceṣṭataḥsvayaṁ ca kṛpaṇaḥ śikṣupaśyann apy abudho ’patat
verses.text: As the father pigeon wretchedly stared at his poor children trapped in the net and on the verge of death, pathetically struggling to free themselves, his mind went blank, and thus he himself fell into the hunter’s net.

verses.sanskrit: तं लब्ध्वा लुब्धक: क्रूर: कपोतं गृहमेधिनम् ।कपोतकान् कपोतीं च सिद्धार्थ: प्रययौ गृहम् ॥ ७२ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ labdhvā lubdhakaḥ krūraḥkapotaṁ gṛha-medhinamkapotakān kapotīṁ casiddhārthaḥ prayayau gṛham
verses.text: The cruel hunter, having fulfilled his desire by capturing the head pigeon, his wife and all of their children, set off for his own home.

verses.sanskrit: एवं कुटुम्ब्यशान्तात्मा द्वन्द्वाराम: पतत्रिवत् ।पुष्णन् कुटुम्बं कृपण: सानुबन्धोऽवसीदति ॥ ७३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ kuṭumby aśāntātmādvandvārāmaḥ patatri-vatpuṣṇan kuṭumbaṁ kṛpaṇaḥsānubandho ’vasīdati
verses.text: In this way, one who is too attached to family life becomes disturbed at heart. Like the pigeon, he tries to find pleasure in mundane sex attraction. Busily engaged in maintaining his own family, the miserly person is fated to suffer greatly, along with all his family members.

verses.sanskrit: य: प्राप्य मानुषं लोकं मुक्तिद्वारमपावृतम् ।गृहेषु खगवत् सक्तस्तमारूढच्युतं विदु: ॥ ७४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaḥ prāpya mānuṣaṁ lokaṁmukti-dvāram apāvṛtamgṛheṣu khaga-vat saktastam ārūḍha-cyutaṁ viduḥ
verses.text: The doors of liberation are opened wide to one who has achieved human life. But if a human being simply devotes himself to family life like the foolish bird in this story, then he is to be considered as one who has climbed to a high place only to trip and fall down.
